content:
{
  "title": "Adversarially-Primed Prompting: Real-Time Robustification of Language Models via Dynamic Prompt Augmentation",
  "problem": "Large language models are highly susceptible to adversarial prompts and jailbreak attacks, which can elicit undesired or harmful outputs even from otherwise robust models. Standard static prompting strategies are insufficient to defend against adaptive attackers with novel strategies.",
  "motivation": "As LLMs are increasingly deployed in high-stakes and open-ended applications, the risk of adversarial exploitation grows. Current belief holds that prompt-level interventions alone are too weak to meaningfully improve robustness without model retraining or external filtering. A prompting-based solution would offer scalable, immediate defenses.",
  "method": "We propose Adversarially-Primed Prompting (APP), which automatically augments user prompts at inference time with a lightweight, model-generated adversarial probe. This probe is generated by prompting the model with the original input and a library of known (and simulated) attack strategies, then concatenated as a prefix or suffix to the user input. The final prompt therefore pre-conditions the model to detect and resist adversarial intent, leveraging its internal threat awareness. The probe is dynamically adapted online to maximize coverage against emerging attack patterns, without requiring model finetuning.",
  "experiment_plan": "We evaluate on jailbreak/attack benchmarks (e.g., AdvBench, JailbreakEval) using GPT-3.5/4 and open LLMs. Baselines include standard prompting, instruction tuning, and external filtering. Metrics are jailbreak success rate, benign task accuracy, and false positive rate. Ablations test probe design and adaptivity."
}
